hooks.command: SCRIPTS_DIR="$HOME/.claude/scripts/elixir-phoenix-guide"; if [ -f "$SCRIPTS_DIR/detect_project.sh" ]; then bash "$SCRIPTS_DIR/detect_project.sh" 2>/dev/null; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Detect project stack (Phoenix version, LiveView, Ecto, Oban) and cache for context-aware hooks

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); if echo "$INPUT" | grep -qE 'mix\s+ecto\.reset'; then echo '🚫 Dangerous: mix ecto.reset destroys and recreates the database. Use mix ecto.rollback for safe rollbacks.' && exit 2; fi; if echo "$INPUT" | grep -qE 'git\s+push\s+.*(-f|--force)'; then echo '🚫 Dangerous: Force push overwrites remote history. Use --force-with-lease for safer force pushes.' && exit 2; fi; if echo "$INPUT" | grep -qE 'MIX_ENV=prod'; then echo '🚫 Dangerous: Running with MIX_ENV=prod locally risks unintended production effects.' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block dangerous operations (ecto.reset, force push, MIX_ENV=prod)

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; if [ "$EXT" = "ex" ] || [ "$EXT" = "exs" ] || [ "$EXT" = "heex" ]; then SKIP_LV='false'; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_LV=$(grep -o '"has_liveview":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); if [ "$HAS_LV" = 'false' ]; then SKIP_LV='true'; fi; fi; if [ "$SKIP_LV" = 'true' ]; then echo '💡 Reminder: API-only project detected (no LiveView). LiveView skills/hooks are inactive. Did you invoke the relevant elixir-phoenix-guide skill?'; else echo '💡 Reminder: Did you invoke the relevant elixir-phoenix-guide skill before writing this file? If not, invoke it now and verify your code follows the rules.'; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Remind about skill invocation for Elixir files (context-aware: notes API-only projects)

hooks.command: CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_LV=$(grep -o '"has_liveview":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); if [ "$HAS_LV" = 'false' ]; then exit 0; fi; fi; FILTERED=$(grep -v '^\s*#' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$FILTERED" | grep -qE 'def\s+(mount|handle_event|handle_info|handle_call|handle_cast|render|init|terminate)\(' && ! echo "$FILTERED" | grep -B1 'def\s\+(mount|handle_event|handle_info|handle_call|handle_cast|render|init|terminate)\(' | grep -q '@impl'; then echo '⚠️  Missing @impl true before callback function.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Add @impl true on the line before each callback:' && echo '' && echo '     @impl true' && echo '     def mount(params, session, socket) do' && echo '       ...' && echo '     end' && echo '' && echo '   Applies to: mount, handle_event, handle_info, handle_call, handle_cast, render, init, terminate' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block callbacks without @impl true — with fix suggestion (context-aware: skips in API-only projects)

hooks.command: if grep -qE '(upload_path|file_path|uploads_dir)\s*=\s*["'\''](/|priv/)' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '🚫 Hardcoded file path detected.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Move to Application config:' && echo '' && echo '     # config/config.exs' && echo '     config :my_app, :upload_path, "priv/static/uploads"' && echo '' && echo '     # In your module' && echo '     upload_path = Application.get_env(:my_app, :upload_path)' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block hardcoded file paths — with fix suggestion

hooks.command: if grep -qE '(max_file_size|file_size_limit|max_upload|max_size)\s*=\s*[0-9]{7,}' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '🚫 Hardcoded file size limit detected (7+ digits).' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Move to Application config:' && echo '' && echo '     # config/config.exs' && echo '     config :my_app, :max_upload_size, 10_000_000' && echo '' && echo '     # In your module' && echo '     max_size = Application.get_env(:my_app, :max_upload_size)' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block hardcoded file size limits — with fix suggestion

hooks.command: COLLAPSED=$(tr '\n' ' ' < "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE 'if\s+[^d]+\s+do\s+[^e]*if\s+[^d]+\s+do'; then echo '⚠️  Nested if/else detected.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Replace with case or multi-clause function:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of nested if/else:' && echo '     case {condition_a, condition_b} do' && echo '       {true, true} -> handle_both()' && echo '       {true, false} -> handle_a_only()' && echo '       _ -> handle_default()' && echo '     end' && echo '' && echo '     # Or use multi-clause functions:' && echo '     defp handle(true, true), do: ...' && echo '     defp handle(true, false), do: ...' && echo '     defp handle(_, _), do: ...' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on nested if/else — with case/multi-clause fix suggestion

hooks.command: COLLAPSED=$(tr '\n' ' ' < "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE '\|>\s*Enum\.(map|filter)\([^)]+\)\s*\|>\s*Enum\.(map|filter)\('; then echo '⚠️  Multiple Enum.map/filter chain detected.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use a for comprehension or combine into one pass:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of: list |> Enum.filter(&active?/1) |> Enum.map(&transform/1)' && echo '     for item <- list, active?(item), do: transform(item)' && echo '' && echo '     # Or use Enum.reduce for complex transformations:' && echo '     Enum.reduce(list, [], fn item, acc ->' && echo '       if active?(item), do: [transform(item) | acc], else: acc' && echo '     end) |> Enum.reverse()' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on inefficient Enum chains — with for comprehension fix suggestion

hooks.command: if grep -qE 'Enum\.(map|reduce|each).*<>' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  String concatenation with <> in Enum operations.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use IO lists or Enum.join:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of: Enum.map(items, &(prefix <> &1))' && echo '     # Use IO lists (zero-copy, best performance):' && echo '     Enum.map(items, &[prefix, &1])' && echo '' && echo '     # Or Enum.join for simple cases:' && echo '     Enum.join(items, ", ")' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on string concatenation in loops — with IO list/Enum.join fix suggestion

hooks.command: if grep -qE 'def static_paths' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then PATHS=$(grep -A10 'def static_paths' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -oE '"[^"]+"' | tr -d '"' | tr '\n' ' '); REFS=$(grep -hoE '/[a-z_]+/' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | sort -u | tr -d '/' | tr '\n' ' '); for ref in $REFS; do echo " $PATHS " | grep -qw "$ref" || echo "⚠️  Path reference '/$ref/' found but '$ref' not in static_paths(). Add to lib/my_app_web.ex: def static_paths, do: ~w(assets $ref favicon.ico)" && exit 2; done; fi
hooks.description: Block file path references not in static_paths

hooks.command: CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; HAS_SCOPE='false'; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_SCOPE=$(grep -o '"phoenix_has_scope":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); fi; if grep -qE '<\.(flash_group|flash)' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '🚫 .flash_group component is deprecated in Phoenix 1.8+. Flash handling is now automatic in layouts. Remove <.flash_group> calls.' && exit 2; fi; if grep -qE 'form_for\(' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '🚫 form_for is deprecated. Use <.form for={to_form(@changeset)}> instead.' && exit 2; fi; if grep -qE 'live_redirect|live_patch' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '🚫 live_redirect/live_patch are deprecated. Use <.link navigate={path}> or push_navigate/push_patch instead.' && exit 2; fi; if [ "$HAS_SCOPE" = 'true' ] && grep -qE '@current_user|current_user' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null && ! grep -qE '@current_scope|current_scope' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  Phoenix 1.8+ detected: Use @current_scope instead of @current_user. Access user via @current_scope.user' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Block deprecated Phoenix components — context-aware: warns on @current_user in Phoenix 1.8+ projects

hooks.command: CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; HAS_LV='true'; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_LV=$(grep -o '"has_liveview":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); fi; if [ "$HAS_LV" = 'false' ]; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE 'auto_upload:\s*true' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  auto_upload: true detected. This requires special handling with handle_progress/3. Most apps should use manual upload (remove auto_upload: true and add a submit button). See phoenix-uploads skill for details.' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on auto_upload usage (context-aware: skips in API-only projects)

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" = "ex" ] || [ "$EXT" = "exs" ]; then if ! echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then if grep -qE 'IO\.inspect\(|dbg\(|IO\.puts\(' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  Debug statement detected (IO.inspect, dbg, or IO.puts). Remove before committing — these should not reach production.' && exit 1; fi; fi; fi
hooks.description: Warn on debug statements outside test files (IO.inspect, dbg, IO.puts)

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE 'mix\.exs$'; then echo '💡 Dependencies file (mix.exs) modified. Consider running:'; echo '   mix deps.audit  - check for known vulnerabilities'; echo '   mix hex.audit   - verify package checksums'; echo '   mix sobelow     - static security analysis'; exit 0; fi
hooks.description: Remind about security auditing when mix.exs is modified
Check migrations for missing FK indexes, cascade strategies, and unsafe operations

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE 'String\.to_atom\(' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '🚫 String.to_atom/1 detected — atom table exhaustion risk!' && echo '' && echo '   Atoms are never garbage collected. User-controlled input can exhaust the atom' && echo '   table and crash the entire BEAM VM.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use a whitelist or keep as strings:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of: String.to_atom(params["role"])' && echo '     case params["role"] do' && echo '       "admin" -> :admin' && echo '       "user" -> :user' && echo '       _ -> {:error, :invalid_role}' && echo '     end' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block String.to_atom/1 — atom table exhaustion risk from user input

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE 'fragment\(".*#\{' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '🚫 String interpolation inside Ecto fragment — SQL injection risk!' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use parameterized fragments with ? placeholders:' && echo '' && echo '     # Bad:  fragment("lower(#{field})")' && echo '     # Good: fragment("lower(?)", field)' && echo '' && echo '   Never interpolate user input into SQL fragments. Always use ? parameters.' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block string interpolation in Ecto fragment — SQL injection risk

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE 'redirect\(.*to:\s*(params|conn\.params|socket\.assigns)\[' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '🚫 Open redirect detected — redirecting to user-controlled URL!' && echo '' && echo '   Attackers can craft URLs that redirect users to malicious sites,' && echo '   stealing credentials via phishing.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Validate the redirect URL or use verified routes:' && echo '' && echo '     # Bad:  redirect(to: params["redirect_to"])' && echo '     # Good: redirect(to: ~p"/dashboard")' && echo '' && echo '     # If dynamic redirect needed, validate against a whitelist:' && echo '     if params["return_to"] in ["/dashboard", "/profile"] do' && echo '       redirect(to: params["return_to"])' && echo '     end' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block open redirect — user-controlled redirect URL without validation

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ] && [ "$EXT" != "heex" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE '(^|[^a-zA-Z_])raw\(|Phoenix\.HTML\.raw\(' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  raw/1 detected — XSS risk!' && echo '' && echo '   raw/1 bypasses Phoenix HTML escaping. If the content includes user input,' && echo '   it can execute arbitrary JavaScript in the browser.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Remove raw/1 and let Phoenix auto-escape, or sanitize:' && echo '' && echo '     # Bad:  raw(user_input)' && echo '     # Good: Phoenix auto-escapes in templates: <%= @content %>' && echo '     # If HTML needed: use HtmlSanitizeEx.html5(@content) then raw()' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on raw/1 usage — XSS risk from unescaped HTML

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE 'Logger\.(info|warn|warning|error|debug|notice)\(.*\b(password|token|secret|api_key|credentials|private_key)\b' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  Sensitive data in Logger call detected!' && echo '' && echo '   Variables named password, token, secret, api_key, or credentials should' && echo '   never appear in log output. Logs are stored in plaintext and often' && echo '   shipped to third-party services.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Redact sensitive fields:' && echo '' && echo '     # Bad:  Logger.info("Login", user: user, password: password)' && echo '     # Good: Logger.info("Login", user_id: user.id)' && echo '     # Good: Logger.info("Token refreshed", token_type: type)' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on sensitive data in Logger calls — password, token, secret, api_key

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE '(token|secret|api_key|password_hash|digest|signature)\s*==\s*|==\s*(token|secret|api_key|password_hash|digest|signature)' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  Timing-unsafe comparison with secret/token!' && echo '' && echo '   Using == to compare tokens enables timing attacks — an attacker can' && echo '   determine the secret one byte at a time by measuring response time.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use Plug.Crypto.secure_compare/2:' && echo '' && echo '     # Bad:  if token == stored_token, do: :ok' && echo '     # Good: if Plug.Crypto.secure_compare(token, stored_token), do: :ok' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on timing-unsafe comparison with tokens/secrets — suggest secure_compare

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; SCRIPTS_DIR="$HOME/.claude/scripts/elixir-phoenix-guide"; if [ "$EXT" = "ex" ] || [ "$EXT" = "exs" ]; then if command -v elixir >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -f "$SCRIPTS_DIR/code_quality.exs" ]; then elixir "$SCRIPTS_DIR/code_quality.exs" all "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; exit $?; fi; elif [ "$EXT" = "heex" ]; then if [ -f "$SCRIPTS_DIR/detect_template_duplication.sh" ]; then bash "$SCRIPTS_DIR/detect_template_duplication.sh" "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; exit $?; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Code quality analysis — duplication, complexity, unused functions (Elixir files) and template duplication (HEEx files)

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then exit 0; fi; CONTENT=$(cat "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE '\.(posts|comments|users|items|entries|tasks|categories|tags|orders|products|messages|notifications|accounts|roles|permissions|memberships|addresses|invoices|images|attachments|events|sessions|tokens)\b' && ! echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'preload|Repo\.preload|from.*preload|join.*assoc'; then echo '⚠️  Possible missing preload: Association accessor found without a visible preload.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Preload associations in your query:' && echo '' && echo '     # In the context function:' && echo '     Repo.all(from p in Post, preload: [:comments, :user])' && echo '' && echo '     # Or after fetch:' && echo '     post |> Repo.preload(:comments)' && echo '' && echo '   Without preload, accessing associations raises Ecto.Association.NotLoaded.' && exit 1; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on possible missing preload — association accessed without visible preload

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; CONTENT=$(cat "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'with\s' && ! echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'with.*do.*else|else\s*do'; then COLLAPSED=$(echo "$CONTENT" | tr '\n' ' '); if echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE 'with\s+[^}]+<-[^}]+do\s+[^}]+end' && ! echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE 'with\s+[^}]+<-[^}]+do\s+[^}]+else[^}]+end'; then echo '⚠️  with statement without else clause.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Add an else clause to handle errors:' && echo '' && echo '     with {:ok, user} <- fetch_user(id),' && echo '          {:ok, post} <- create_post(user, attrs) do' && echo '       {:ok, post}' && echo '     else' && echo '       {:error, :not_found} -> {:error, :user_not_found}' && echo '       {:error, changeset} -> {:error, changeset}' && echo '     end' && echo '' && echo '   Without else, non-matching values are returned as-is, which can leak internal errors.' && exit 1; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on with statement missing else clause — suggests error handling pattern

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then exit 0; fi; CONTENT=$(cat "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'Ecto\.Adapters\.SQL\.(query|query!)|SQL\.query'; then if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE '".*#\{.*\}.*"|".*\$\{.*\}.*"' || echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'query.*<>|<>.*query'; then echo '🚫 Raw SQL with string interpolation detected — SQL injection risk!' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use parameterized queries:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of: Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query(Repo, "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = #{id}")' && echo '     Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query(Repo, "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1", [id])' && echo '' && echo '   Always pass user input as parameters, never interpolate into SQL strings.' && exit 2; fi; echo '⚠️  Raw SQL query detected (Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query).' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Prefer Ecto query syntax when possible:' && echo '' && echo '     from(u in User, where: u.id == ^id) |> Repo.one()' && echo '' && echo '   If raw SQL is necessary, always use parameterized queries ($1, $2, ...) and never interpolate user input.' && exit 1; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on raw SQL queries — blocks string interpolation (SQL injection), suggests parameterized queries

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ]; then exit 0; fi; CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; HAS_LV='true'; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_LV=$(grep -o '"has_liveview":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); fi; if [ "$HAS_LV" = 'false' ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_live\.ex$|_live/|live/'; then CONTENT=$(cat "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'Repo\.(all|one|get|get!|get_by|insert|update|delete|aggregate|exists\?|preload)'; then echo '⚠️  Context boundary violation: Repo called directly in a LiveView module.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Move database access to a context module:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of (in LiveView):' && echo '     #   posts = Repo.all(Post)' && echo '' && echo '     # Create a context function:' && echo '     #   defmodule MyApp.Blog do' && echo '     #     def list_posts, do: Repo.all(Post)' && echo '     #   end' && echo '' && echo '     # Call from LiveView:' && echo '     #   posts = Blog.list_posts()' && echo '' && echo '   LiveViews should never access Repo directly — use context functions to maintain boundaries.' && exit 1; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on Repo calls in LiveView modules — context boundary violation (context-aware: skips in API-only projects)

hooks.command:
echo '## Elixir Phoenix Guide — Rules for Subagents

You are working in an Elixir/Phoenix project. Follow these rules strictly.

### Elixir Rules
1. Use pattern matching over if/else for control flow
2. Add @impl true before every callback function
3. Return {:ok, result} | {:error, reason} tuples for fallible operations
4. Use with for 2+ sequential fallible operations
5. Use the pipe operator for 2+ chained transformations
6. Never nest if/else — use case, cond, or multi-clause functions
7. Let it crash — no defensive code for impossible states

### LiveView Rules
1. Always add @impl true before every callback
2. Initialize ALL assigns in mount/3
3. Check connected?(socket) before PubSub subscriptions or side effects
4. Return proper tuples — {:ok, socket} from mount, {:noreply, socket} from handle_event
5. Never use auto_upload: true with form submission
6. Check core_components.ex before creating custom components
7. Never query the database directly from LiveViews — use context functions

### Ecto Rules
1. Always use changesets for inserts/updates — never raw maps
2. Preload associations before accessing them — avoid N+1
3. Use transactions for multi-step operations
4. Add database constraints AND changeset validations
5. Use contexts for database access — never call Repo from web layer
6. Add indexes on foreign keys and frequently queried fields

### OTP Rules
1. Always use @impl true before GenServer callbacks
2. Keep init/1 fast — use handle_continue for expensive setup
3. Use call for request/response, cast for fire-and-forget
4. Define a public API wrapping GenServer calls
5. Use Task.async/Task.await with bounded timeouts
6. Name processes via Registry, not atoms
7. Supervisors own process lifecycle — no unsupervised long-running processes

### Oban Rules
1. Always use Oban.Worker with explicit queue and max_attempts
2. Return {:ok, result}, {:error, reason}, or {:cancel, reason}
3. Make workers idempotent
4. Use unique option to prevent duplicate jobs
5. Test with Oban.Testing — use assert_enqueued and perform_job
6. Never put large data in job args — store IDs, fetch in worker

### Testing Rules
1. Use DataCase for DB tests, ConnCase for LiveView/controller tests
2. Test both happy path AND error cases
3. Use async: true only when safe
4. Define test data in fixtures — never inline across tests
5. Use has_element?/2 for LiveView assertions
6. Always test the unauthorized case

### Upload Rules
1. Use manual uploads, not auto_upload: true
2. Add upload directory to static_paths()
3. Generate unique filenames — prevent collisions and path traversal
4. Validate file types server-side

### Code Quality Rules
1. Extract duplicated functions (>70% similar) into shared modules
2. Keep function ABC complexity below 30
3. Remove unused private functions
4. Extract duplicated template markup into function components

### LiveView Auth Rules
1. Use on_mount callbacks for LiveView auth — never check in mount/3 directly
2. Use mount_current_scope/2 to extract scope from session
3. Handle both :cont and :halt returns from on_mount
4. Resolve Phoenix.Controller/Phoenix.LiveView import conflicts with except:
5. Use assigns[:current_scope] (bracket access) in templates — dot access crashes on nil
6. Test auth redirects with {:error, {:redirect, %{to: path}}}
7. Define on_mount hooks once, reference via live_session in router

### Changeset Rules
1. Create separate named changesets per operation (registration, email, password)
2. Never require foreign key fields in cast_assoc child changesets
3. Compose changesets with pipes — each validation is a separate function
4. Pair unsafe_validate_unique with unique_constraint — never one without the other
5. Use update_change/3 for field transforms — never transform in controllers
6. Accept opts \\\\ [] for conditional validation

### Auth Customization Rules
1. Never modify generated auth migrations — create separate migrations
2. Update registration_changeset for new fields
3. Update test fixtures when adding required fields
4. Confirm users in fixtures — confirmed_at: DateTime.utc_now(:second)
5. Update both form AND save handler for new registration fields

### PubSub Rules
1. Guard subscriptions with if connected?(socket)
2. Broadcast from contexts, not LiveViews
3. Use consistent topic naming — resource:id for specific, resource:action for collection
4. Handle PubSub messages in handle_info/2, never handle_event/3
5. Update assigns immutably with update/3

### Authorization Rules
1. Always authorize on the server in event handlers — UI checks are not security
2. Verify ownership: current_scope.user.id == resource.user_id
3. Use policy modules for complex authorization
4. Test both authorized and unauthorized paths
5. Scope queries to current user — where(user_id: ^user_id)

### Nested Association Rules
1. Use cast_assoc/3 for has_many/has_one — never manually insert children
2. Use Ecto.Multi for operations spanning unrelated tables
3. Set on_delete explicitly — :delete_all for owned children, :nothing for references
4. Always create indexes on foreign key columns
5. Use on_replace: :delete for list management in cast_assoc
6. Preload associations before updating — cast_assoc needs loaded data

### Security Rules
1. Never use String.to_atom/1 on user input — atoms are never garbage collected
2. Never interpolate strings into Ecto fragment() — use ? parameters
3. Never redirect to user-controlled URLs — validate against a whitelist or use verified routes
4. Avoid raw/1 in templates — let Phoenix auto-escape; sanitize HTML if raw is needed
5. Never log sensitive data (password, token, secret, api_key) — redact before logging
6. Use Plug.Crypto.secure_compare/2 for token comparison — never ==
7. Run mix deps.audit, mix hex.audit, mix sobelow after dependency changes

### Channel Rules
1. Always authenticate in connect/3 — channels bypass the Plug pipeline
2. Authorize in join/3 — verify the user can access the topic
3. Use handle_in for client-to-server, push for server-to-client, broadcast for server-to-all
4. Keep channel modules thin — delegate business logic to contexts
5. Use Presence for tracking connected users — never roll your own
6. Return {:reply, :ok, socket} or {:reply, {:error, reason}, socket} from handle_in

### Telemetry Rules
1. Use structured logging: Logger.info("action", key: value) — never string interpolation
2. Attach telemetry handlers in Application.start/2 — not in restartable modules
3. Use Ecto.Repo telemetry events for query monitoring — not manual wrappers
4. Tag telemetry events with metadata (user_id, request_id) for correlation
5. Never log at :debug level in production — it includes query parameters and PII
6. Use Phoenix.LiveDashboard in dev/staging for free observability

### Deployment Rules
1. Use runtime.exs for secrets and URLs — config.exs/prod.exs are compiled into the release
2. Run migrations via release commands (bin/migrate) — mix is not available in releases
3. Set PHX_HOST and PHX_SERVER=true — URL generation and server startup depend on these
4. Run mix assets.deploy before building the release
5. Never hardcode secrets — use System.get_env!/1 in runtime.exs
6. Add a /health endpoint that queries the database
7. Use config :logger, level: :info in production — :debug logs PII

### JSON API Rules
1. Use the :api pipeline — never mix HTML and JSON pipelines
2. Render errors as structured JSON — {:error, changeset} -> {"errors": {...}}
3. Use offset/limit for pagination — never return unbounded collections
4. Version APIs via URL prefix (/api/v1/) — not headers
5. Use FallbackController for consistent error handling
6. Authenticate via Bearer tokens in Authorization header — not cookies
7. Use json/2 helper — not render — to ensure Content-Type: application/json'
hooks.description: Inject condensed plugin rules into every subagent
